summary: Excellent analog audience recording captures a jubilant show with scorching Let It Grow and a stellar post‑Space, plus an ace Jerry solo and the rare Might As Well encore.
review: The upgraded 24‑bit Jim Vita audience tape, mastered by Scott Clugston and transferred by Charlie Miller, delivers crystal‑clear analog warmth. Musically the concert is fire‑baked from start to finish – Let It Grow shines as the crown jewel, The Other One rolls perfectly, Estimated Prophet > Eyes of the World rockets at breakneck speed, and a post‑Space segues into a joyous Sugar Magnolia capped by a one‑time encore of Might As Well.

ai_rating.rationale: The single detailed review is overwhelmingly positive about both performance and the high‑quality audience recording, justifying a strong rating despite limited sample size.

recording_quality.technical_notes: 24‑bit upgrade of Jim Vita's analog audience tape, mastering by Scott Clugston and transfer by Charlie Miller; described as 'stellar' and representative of the golden age of analog audience recordings.

standout_songs: Estimated Prophet > Eyes of the World
show_quality.setlist_flow: Both sets are praised as tight and fun, with a poetic first‑set flow and a second set that covers all bases, culminating in an exceptional post‑Space segment.

band_member_comments.Jerry: Pulled an ace with a raucous solo during the encore.
band_member_comments.Bob: Delivered a powerful solo on Sugar Magnolia.
